title: eulophia woodfordii
intro: Eulophia woodfordii is a species of orchid, commonly known as the Woodford's eulophia, native to Africa. Traditionally, the plant has been used in various cultures for its medicinal properties, which include anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial activities. The health benefits of Eulophia woodfordii are said to include wound healing, reduction of fever, and relief from respiratory issues such as coughs and bronchitis. In herbal preparations, the plant is often dried and used in teas or infusions, or made into a paste for topical application, although further research is needed to confirm its efficacy and safety.
